compiled_state.title: Create SIGPIPE canary test demonstrating pipefail divergence detection
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Story DD Coverage
This task satisfies:
- DD3: Synthetic SIGPIPE-class regression test (canary) demonstrates divergence detection works

## Description
Create tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh — a permanent canary test demonstrating that bash
pipefail catches pipeline failures that normal bash silently swallows.

This test must FAIL (RED) before creation (no file exists), then PASS after creation.

### Test content

The canary uses the `false | true` pattern — a platform-independent, SIGPIPE-free demonstration:
- Without pipefail: `false | true` exits 0 (last command's exit code wins)
- With pipefail: `false | true` exits 1 (first failure propagates through the pipeline)

This is simpler and more reliable than triggering an actual SIGPIPE (which has platform variance).

### Test functions to implement:

**test_canary_file_executable**: Script itself is executable (meta-test that verifies test infrastructure)

**test_pipefail_catches_pipeline_failure**:
- Given: `bash -eo pipefail -c 'false | true'`
- When: executed
- Then: exits non-zero (pipefail propagates `false` failure through the pipeline)
- Contrasted with: `bash -c 'false | true'` which exits 0 (normal bash, last-cmd-wins)
This documents the divergence mechanically.

**test_bash_opts_exported_env**:
- Given: BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail' is in the environment
- When: a subshell inherits it
- Then: the subshell runs with pipefail semantics
This mimics what the CI job does — BASH_OPTS is a job-level env var that bash picks up automatically.

### File: tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh

Use the same testing harness as other test files in tests/scripts/:
- Source tests/lib/assert.sh for assert_eq, print_summary
- Include _snapshot_fail / assert_pass_if_clean wrappers per existing convention
- Exit 0 when all pass, exit 1 on any failure
- Add `#!/usr/bin/env bash` and set -uo pipefail header

### .test-index entry
Add to .test-index with RED marker (canary file doesn't exist yet):
`tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh:tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh [test_canary_file_executable]`

Note: source and test are the same file (self-testing pattern used by other test files in the index).
The fuzzy-match algorithm: normalized 'testsigpipecanarysh' IS a substring of 'testsigpipecanarysh' — fuzzy-matchable. Still add .test-index entry because the file doesn't exist yet (RED gate).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh
- [ ] bash tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh passes (PASSED: N FAILED: 0)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh && echo OK
- [ ] test_pipefail_catches_pipeline_failure function present
  Verify: grep -q 'test_pipefail_catches_pipeline_failure' tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh
- [ ] test demonstrates that `false | true` exits non-zero under pipefail
  Verify: bash -eo pipefail -c 'false | true'; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] test demonstrates that `false | true` exits zero without pipefail
  Verify: bash -c 'false | true'; [ $? -eq 0 ]
- [ ] .test-index entry added for tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh with RED marker [test_canary_file_executable]
  Verify: grep 'test-sigpipe-canary.sh.*\[test_canary_file_executable\]' .test-index
- [ ] ruff check passes (skip if no .py files modified)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py 2>/dev/null || true

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-sigpipe-canary.sh — CREATE
- .test-index — MODIFY (add entry with RED marker)

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
